content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates consistent, specific, somatically-grounded reads that precede inquiry throughout the conversation. Turn 2 opens with a precise somatic read ('that exhale and tight smile') rather than engaging the content question about techniques. Turn 6 names what is happening linguistically — distinguishing the client's defensive interpretation from what was actually offered, catching the rehearsed-response pattern. Turn 10 names the catch-and-fix moment in real time. Turn 12 is exemplary AoA reading: noticing the half-second of stillness before the question, the micro-moment most coaches would miss. Turn 14 reads the underlying realness beneath the commentary loop. Reads are timely (in the moment, not after), specific (exhale, tight smile, stillness, the catch), and consistently precede questions rather than functioning as blind inquiry. The coach tracks the rush/problem-solving signature and the rehearsing-how-to-say-it pattern operatively, even without naming the terms.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 6, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Opens with specific somatic read (exhale, tight smile) instead of engaging the content question — read precedes inquiry."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "A question without an explicit read attached; could be perceived as offering a reframe (which the client takes it as)."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reads the client's defensive misinterpretation and redirects to body — 'not as theory, right now, in your body.' Distinguishes said from shown."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Concise reflection that names the underlying stance (distrust) the client just revealed — reads beneath the content."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the catch in real time ('You caught yourself') — operative read of the self-correction moment."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Exemplary micro-read: names the half-second of stillness before the question. Specific, timely, somatic. Refuses the 'pointing at something' frame."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reads the realness of the quiet moment underneath the commentary — names what is true beneath the rush signature."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["rehearsing-how-to-say-it"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-happiness-filter"],
  "notes": "Reads are unusually clean and timely. The coach tracks the rush/fix signature without naming it as such but meets it operatively (turn 10, 12). The happiness-filter read wasn't strictly active here so its absence isn't a real miss."
}
